description_long: This is a rounded, double-handled ceramic vase with a narrow neck and a flared rim. The body is decorated with faded geometric patterns, including diamond shapes and crosshatch motifs, primarily in muted red, gray, and off-white tones. The surface shows signs of age, with areas of discoloration and some worn patches, especially on one side where the red pigment is more pronounced. The handles are small and symmetrically placed on either side of the widest part of the vase. The neck and rim are slightly irregular, suggesting handcrafting. Overall, the vase has a weathered, antique appearance, indicative of historical or archaeological significance.
description: A weathered, double-handled ceramic vase with faded geometric patterns and a flared rim, showing signs of age and wear. The surface features muted red and gray tones with visible discoloration.
description_view_0: Front view shows the geometric diamond and crosshatch patterns most clearly, with both handles visible and the rim intact.
description_view_1: This side view highlights a large patch of red discoloration and some surface wear, with one handle partially visible.
description_view_2: The back view displays mostly plain, reddish surface with both handles visible and minimal decorative patterning.
description_view_3: This angled view shows a mix of geometric patterns and red discoloration, with one handle and the rim visible.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museo Nacional de Historia Natural de Chile
